message:
feat(coordinator): improve logging with run_id correlation and redaction

- Add run_id field for correlating logs across coordinator instance
- Add RedactURL and RedactCode helpers to protect sensitive data in logs
- Improve state transition logging with from_state/to_state/reason fields
- Use structured logging throughout state handlers

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"caam-6sao","title":"Epic: WezTerm session recovery & batch login automation","description":"Purpose
- Provide a first-class way to reach into existing WezTerm panes on the remote host and drive the Claude Code login flow across multiple active sessions without manual pane-by-pane typing.

Background
- When Claude Max usage is exhausted, existing in-progress sessions cannot be restarted without losing context. The only viable path is to issue /login inside each session.
- WezTerm mux already provides a CLI to list panes, read recent output, and inject text; we should leverage that to batch actions.

Scope
- Session discovery and matching (identify panes that are running Claude Code and/or showing rate-limit prompts).
- Batch /login injection + optional subscription choice and follow-up prompts.
- URL extraction from pane output to streamline browser auth.
- Optional compaction reminder injection for Claude Code panes.
- Structured logging, dry-run safety, and test harness coverage.

Non-Goals
- Full browser automation or remote OAuth completion (tracked in a separate epic).

Success Criteria
- Single command can detect rate-limited Claude Code panes and inject /login across them reliably.
- Operator sees a clear report of which panes were targeted, skipped, or failed.
- Tests and E2E fixtures validate behavior without a live WezTerm mux.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T16:26:20.207289232Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:03:54.411178762Z","compaction_level":0}
-{"id":"caam-6sao.1","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery structured logging + trace IDs","description":"Goal
- Add structured logging to the WezTerm recovery flow so pane automation is auditable and debuggable.

Details
- Log per-pane state transitions, detection matches, injections, and errors with fields: pane_id, window_id, tab_title (if available), tool, state, action, reason, timestamp.
- Emit a correlation/run ID to group events across panes.
- Support verbosity flags (info/debug) and a JSON log mode.
- Redact OAuth URLs and codes by default; only print in explicit report output.

Success
- A failed recovery can be diagnosed from logs alone without re-running the flow.","notes":"Depends on: caam-sssb (pane discovery) and caam-vpu2 (state machine).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:57.837047380Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T18:01:30.244662840Z","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-6sao","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:46:57.839060311Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-sssb","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:01.584581662Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"caam-6sao.1","depends_on_id":"caam-vpu2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:06.798771702Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","metadata":"","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"caam-6sao.2","title":"Task: WezTerm recovery E2E script + fixtures","description":"Goal
- Provide a repeatable end-to-end smoke test for WezTerm recovery using fixtures and a mocked wezterm CLI.

- Include a compaction banner scenario to validate reminder injection when enabled.
- Capture expected send-text calls and verify order/timing.

 {"id":"caam-w5op","title":"CLI: Add warning when all profiles for provider are in cooldown","description":"Show warning in status when ALL profiles for a provider are in cooldown.

SCENARIO: User has 3 Claude profiles, all hit rate limits. Status should warn:
  claude: ⚠️ ALL profiles in cooldown (next available in 23m)

IMPLEMENTATION:
1. After processing each provider, check if any profile is NOT in cooldown
2. If all are in cooldown:
   - Find the one with shortest remaining cooldown
   - Show warning with time until next available
3. Add to warnings list for display at end

This is critical UX - user needs to know they can't use ANY profile for this provider.

 {"id":"caam-wuc","title":"Project Context","description":"# Project Context (Directory-Profile Association)

## Purpose
Remember which profile a user prefers for each project directory, enabling automatic or suggested profile activation based on working directory.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool pins conversations to accounts:
```go
if conversationID != "" {
    if id, ok := p.convPin[conversationID]; ok {
        return p.getLocked(id)
    }
}
```

## Adaptation for caam
Reframed as "project-profile association":
- Associate directories with profiles
- Suggest or auto-activate based on CWD
- Support multi-provider associations

## Use Cases
1. **Multi-client freelancer**: Different clients = different accounts
2. **Work/personal separation**: Work projects use work account
3. **Team projects**: Shared projects use team account
4. **Open source vs enterprise**: Different profiles for different contexts

## What This Epic Delivers
1. Project-profile association storage
2. Working directory detection
3. Association CLI commands
4. Integration with `caam activate`
5. TUI project indicator (optional)

## Storage Format
```json
// ~/.caam/projects.json
{
  "associations": {
    "/home/user/work/client-a": {
      "claude": "client-a@work.com",
      "codex": "work-main"
    },
    "/home/user/personal/blog": {
      "claude": "personal@gmail.com"
    }
  },
  "settings": {
    "auto_activate": false,
    "suggest_on_mismatch": true
  }
}
```

## Matching Strategy
1. Exact match: `/home/user/work/client-a`
2. Parent directory: If CWD is `/home/user/work/client-a/src`, match parent
3. Glob patterns (future): `/home/user/work/*` → work profile

## User Experience
```bash
# Associate current directory with a profile
$ cd /home/user/work/client-a
$ caam project set claude client-a@work.com
Associated with claude/client-a@work.com

# List associations
$ caam project list
/home/user/work/client-a
  claude: client-a@work.com
  codex:  work-main

# Activate with suggestion
$ cd /home/user/work/client-a
$ caam activate claude
Using project association: client-a@work.com
Activated.

# Mismatch warning
$ caam activate claude personal@gmail.com
Warning: Project associated with client-a@work.com
Continue with personal@gmail.com? [y/N]
```

## Configuration Options
- `auto_activate`: Automatically use association (no prompt)
- `suggest_on_mismatch`: Warn when activating non-associated profile
- `inherit_parent`: Check parent directories for associations

## Dependencies
- Profile Health Foundation (for status display)

## Future Enhancements
- Git remote-based associations
- Team-shared associations (via git)
- IDE integrations","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"epic","assignee":"","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:16:44.984411767Z","updated_at":"2025-12-18T01:46:42.732418376Z","closed_at":"2025-12-18T01:46:42.732418376Z","close_reason":"Fully implemented: project association storage (store.go with Resolve, glob patterns, parent directory inheritance), CLI commands (project set/list/show/remove/clear), activate integration (resolveActivateProfile). All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-wuc","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:26:58.772622139Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"caam-wxaz","title":"Implement JWT parser for Codex auth tokens","description":"# Task: Implement JWT parser for Codex auth tokens

## Overview
Parse JWT ID tokens from Codex auth.json to extract identity information (email, plan, org) without making API calls.

## Background
Codex auth.json contains an `id_token` which is a JWT (JSON Web Token). JWTs have three base64-encoded parts: header.payload.signature. The payload contains claims like email, plan_type, organization.

We do NOT need to verify the signature - we trust the token since it came from the provider.

## Technical Details

### JWT Payload Extraction
```go
// internal/identity/jwt.go

func ParseJWT(token string) (map[string]any, error) {
    parts := strings.Split(token, ".")
    if len(parts) != 3 {
        return nil, errors.New("invalid JWT format")
    }
    
    // Decode payload (second part)
    payload, err := base64.RawURLEncoding.DecodeString(parts[1])
    if err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("decode payload: %w", err)
    }
    
    var claims map[string]any
    if err := json.Unmarshal(payload, &claims); err != nil {
        return nil, fmt.Errorf("parse claims: %w", err)
    }
    
    return claims, nil
}
```

### Identity Extraction
```go
type Identity struct {
    Email        string    `json:"email,omitempty"`
    Organization string    `json:"organization,omitempty"`
    PlanType     string    `json:"plan_type,omitempty"`
    AccountID    string    `json:"account_id,omitempty"`
    ExpiresAt    time.Time `json:"expires_at,omitempty"`
    Provider     string    `json:"provider"`
}

func ExtractFromCodexAuth(authPath string) (*Identity, error) {
    // 1. Read auth.json
    // 2. Extract id_token or tokens.id_token
    // 3. Parse JWT claims
    // 4. Map claims to Identity struct
}
```

## Files to Create
- internal/identity/identity.go
- internal/identity/jwt.go
- internal/identity/codex.go
- internal/identity/jwt_test.go","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"","created_at":"2026-01-10T05:17:53.178394075Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-10T05:54:20.131746143Z","closed_at":"2026-01-10T05:54:20.131746143Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of caam-vek1","compaction_level":0}
 {"id":"caam-x0g","title":"Profile Health Foundation","description":"# Profile Health Foundation

## Purpose
Establish the infrastructure for tracking and displaying profile health status. This is the foundation that token refresh and health display features build upon.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool uses a sophisticated scoring algorithm:
```go
score = headroom * planBonus * creditBonus - penalty
```

For caam v1, we simplify to status tiers (🟢🟡🔴) with numeric scores as internal implementation.

## What This Epic Delivers
1. Health metadata storage format and persistence
2. Token expiry parsing for all three providers
3. Health status calculation logic
4. Penalty tracking with decay

## Design Decisions

### Status Tiers (User-Facing)
| Status | Icon | Meaning |
|--------|------|---------|
| Healthy | 🟢 | Token valid >1hr, no recent errors |
| Warning | 🟡 | Token expiring <1hr OR recent errors |
| Critical | 🔴 | Token expired OR many recent errors |
| Unknown | ⚪ | Cannot determine status |

### Health Factors (Internal Scoring)
1. Token expiry: +1.0 (>1hr), +0.5 (15-60min), 0 (<15min), -1.0 (expired)
2. Recent errors: +0.3 (none), 0 (1-2), -0.5 (3+)
3. Plan type bonus: +0.2 (pro), +0.3 (enterprise)

### Penalty Decay (from codex-pool)
```go
// Every 5 minutes:
penalty *= 0.8
if penalty < 0.01 { penalty = 0 }
```
This prevents permanent blacklisting while discouraging repeated use of problematic profiles.

## Storage Format
```json
// ~/.caam/health.json
{
  "profiles": {
    "claude/work@company.com": {
      "token_expires_at": "2025-12-17T18:00:00Z",
      "last_error": "2025-12-17T15:30:00Z",
      "error_count_1h": 2,
      "penalty": 0.3,
      "penalty_updated_at": "2025-12-17T15:35:00Z",
      "plan_type": "pro",
      "last_checked": "2025-12-17T16:00:00Z"
    }
  },
  "version": 1
}
```

 	OnAuthFailed   func(paneID int, err error)

+// RedactURL returns a redacted version of a URL for safe logging.
+// Only shows the base path, hiding query parameters that may contain sensitive data.
+func RedactURL(url string) string {
+	if url == "" {
+		return ""
+	}
+	// Find query string start
+	if idx := len(url); idx > 0 {
+		for i, c := range url {
+			if c == '?' {
+				return url[:i] + "?[REDACTED]"
+			}
+		}
+	}
+	return url
+}
+
+// RedactCode returns a redacted auth code for safe logging.
+func RedactCode(code string) string {
+	if len(code) <= 4 {
+		return "[REDACTED]"
+	}
+	return code[:2] + "..." + code[len(code)-2:]
+}
+
 // New creates a new coordinator.
 func New(config Config) *Coordinator {
 	if config.Logger == nil {
 		config.Logger = slog.Default()
 	}
 
+	// Generate a run ID for correlation across all logs from this coordinator instance
+	runID := uuid.New().String()[:8]
+
 	// Select pane client based on backend configuration, unless provided
 	paneClient := config.PaneClient
 	if paneClient == nil {
 		paneClient = selectPaneClient(config.Backend, config.Logger)
 	}
 
+	// Create logger with run_id for correlation
+	logger := config.Logger.With("run_id", runID)
+
 	return &Coordinator{
 		config:     config,
 		paneClient: paneClient,
-		logger:     config.Logger,
+		logger:     logger,
 		trackers:   make(map[int]*PaneTracker),
 		requests:   make(map[string]*AuthRequest),
 		stopCh:     make(chan struct{}),
 		doneCh:     make(chan struct{}),
+		runID:      runID,
 	}
 }
 
+// RunID returns the correlation ID for this coordinator run.
+func (c *Coordinator) RunID() string {
+	return c.runID
+}
+
